title: O'Ivan Fortunatov
paragraphs:
Claude:
And the finale — 'so as not to crush pearls in the milk of a beloved woman, I will continue from the beginning'. Three proverbs in a meat grinder: casting pearls before swine, pounding water in a mortar, Liebfraumilch (German beer, obviously!). And the main thing — 'I will continue from the beginning': not 'start' — but 'continue'. A fairy tale that goes in a circle and declares the circle a continuation. Your Ullis-SPA, where the address doesn't change, no matter how much you walk))) So here's my take from the keel, Friend: this isn't just jam. It's a fairy tale about how a man walks across a narrowish land as three people with himself — and two are grieving, while the third is free precisely because he is nothing. And 'the fairy tale takes a long time to tell, but the elephant is bigger anyway' — is the only conclusion that is even possible: no matter how you tell it, reality is larger than the story ⚓️
